task: CORAL RNA-protein INTERACTION detection (binary), component-wise cold-start split

provenance.env: torch 2.8+cu128 (the pinned 2.6+cu124 lacked compatible GPU kernels -> 'no kernel image')
provenance.protocol_pathA: per-fold retrain: Data/datasets/component-wise/fold_{k}/{train,val}.csv, batch 4, lr 1e-6, bf16, LoRA rank 8; ESM-2-150M + DNABERT2-117M + bidirectional cross-attention
provenance.note: Numbers below are DISPLAYED here (not recomputed from a clone) because they need the CORAL repo + its datasets + a GPU. This node re-plots them; recompute on a GPU node with the protocol above.

splits.pairwise: held pairs unseen but proteins/RNAs may recur
splits.component_wise: 0% protein AND 0% RNA overlap with train = the TRUE cold-start; the decisive number

released_checkpoint_reused_across_folds.note: LEAKS: 15-23% pair overlap across the released folds; own-fold clean still 0.94 => the released checkpoint was trained on a broader set, not honest per-fold CV

clean_per_fold_retrain_pathA.note: HONEST reproduction: retrain per fold on that fold's train.csv only, evaluate that fold's val.csv

external_eclip_through_coral.note: our Moyon eCLIP -> CORAL CSV (adapters/coral.py) run through CORAL: ~chance -- window-occupancy is a DIFFERENT task from RNA-protein interaction

verdict: Component-wise is a genuine cold-start (0% protein/RNA overlap). The single released checkpoint leaks across folds (F1 0.92); a clean per-fold retrain reproduces ~0.57, below the paper's 0.65. CORAL stands as an existence proof that INTERACTION/family diversity enables cold-start generalization -- which is exactly the motivation for our independent-family scaling curve (paralog/window scaling is flat; independent-family count is the lever).
